text.title: dora-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for dora-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using DORA metrics for individual performance reviews or team comparison without context.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DORA measures team/system outcomes, not individual contribution. Teams with different domain complexity, technical debt, or size have incomparable baseline metrics. Punitive use causes gaming: teams split large deployments into trivial changes to inflate Deployment Frequency.

failure-mode.description: Optimizing Deployment Frequency without watching Change Failure Rate.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deploying more often with inadequate test coverage raises CFR. High-frequency + high-CFR = more outages, not better delivery. The metrics must be tracked and improved together.

failure-mode.description: Setting targets based on industry benchmarks without establishing your own baseline first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: An organization at Low performance cannot jump to Elite. Setting unrealistic targets creates pressure to game metrics rather than improve systems. Set targets as 20-30% improvement from current baseline, not as absolute Elite thresholds.
